question_text: Explain the difference between a mixture and a compound.
correct_answer: A compound is formed when elements are chemically bonded together in fixed proportions and cannot be easily separated. A mixture is formed when two or more substances are combined but not chemically bonded, and can be separated by physical methods.
explanation: Compounds have a fixed chemical formula and specific properties. Mixtures can have varying proportions and their properties depend on the ratio of the substances. For example, salt and water form a mixture (salt dissolves but isn't chemically bonded), while salt (NaCl) is a compound.
